insight: codex-plugin-cc is slash commands + codex-rescue subagent + hooks wrapping the global codex CLI, not MCP. Claude-side CLAUDE.md never reaches Codex subprocesses (shared standards must live in AGENTS.md / repo files). The optional stop-review feature causes open-ended review loops and drains usage; keep it off in pipelines.

gstack, mattpocock/skills, and the symphony-forge factory are three competing full pipelines; ponytail deletion-reviews fight CAW NestJS boilerplate and the success/data/error envelope; openclaw autoreview is the one composable piece. Curate per-phase owners in harness.yaml before installing any pack.

Codex CLI sandbox write-protects the .agents/ directory in BOTH the plugin job runner and direct codex exec workspace-write mode (mkdir/patch/symlink all denied) — it is where Codex discovers its own skills/config. Any harness work that writes .agents/ must be done by Claude Code or a human; Codex can still write everywhere else in the repo except .git (plugin jobs also cannot commit).
